title: Instagram head pressed on lengthy delay to launch teen safety features, like a nudity filter, court filing reveals
description: An email chain with Instagram head Adam Mosseri indicated the company was aware of teen safety issues in DMs in 2018, but didn't launch its unwanted nudity filter until 2024.

title: More startups are hitting $10M ARR in 3 months than ever before
description: AI has brought the startup world the rise of startups that instantly hit multimillion ARR. Stripe revealed some data that shows how common this has become.

title: Instagram’s TV app is launching on Google TV devices
description: By bringing Reels to TVs, Instagram looking to better compete with YouTube, which largely dominates the TV space.

title: Treasury sanctions Russian zero-day broker accused of buying exploits stolen from U.S. defense contractor
description: The U.S. Treasury announced it was imposing sanctions against a Russian broker of zero-day exploits, its founder and two affiliates, citing a threat to U.S. national security. Another affiliated zero-day broker in the United Arab Emirates was also sanctioned.

title: OpenAI COO says ‘we have not yet really seen AI penetrate enterprise business processes’
description: There is a lot of talk around AI agents taking over business processes and claiming that "SaaS is dead." While these predictions have moved SaaS stocks at times, they haven't really come true.

title: YouTube beefs up its $7.99/month Lite subscription with offline downloads and background play
description: Now the only reason to get the full Premium subscription is ad-free music and music videos.

title: Music generator ProducerAI joins Google Labs
description: Wyclef Jean used Google's AI music tools on his new song "Back in Abu Dhabi."

title: Marquis sues firewall provider SonicWall, alleges security failings with its firewall backup led to ransomware attack
description: Fintech giant Marquis is suing its firewall provider SonicWall, claiming that an earlier breach with SonicWall allowed hackers to deploy ransomware on Marquis' network.

title: Ukraine’s startups keep building
description: In the four years since Russia’s full-scale invasion of their country, Ukrainian startups have done more than survive: they are still building and growing.

title: Meta strikes up to $100B AMD chip deal as it chases ‘personal superintelligence’
description: Meta is buying billions of dollars in AMD AI chips in a multiyear deal tied to a 160 million-share warrant, deepening its push to diversify beyond Nvidia and expand data center capacity.

title: Oura launches a proprietary AI model focused on women’s health
description: The model supports questions spanning the full reproductive health spectrum, from early menstrual cycles through menopause.

title: Mogul says it has tracked $1.5B in music royalties, raised $5M in funding
description: Mogul, which helps artists track royalties and value their catalogs, raised $5 million in a round led by the Yamaha Music Innovations Fund.

title: Seedance 2.0 might be gen AI video’s next big hope, but it’s still slop
Archer Aviation accuses rival Vertical Aerospace of ripping off its air taxi designs
Anthropic’s Claude Cowork is plugging AI into more boring enterprise stuff
